Package software.amazon.lambda.durable.operation

Class ParallelOperation<T>

java.lang.Object
software.amazon.lambda.durable.operation.BaseDurableOperation<T>
software.amazon.lambda.durable.operation.ConcurrencyOperation<T>
software.amazon.lambda.durable.operation.ParallelOperation<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the result type of this operation (typically Void)
All Implemented Interfaces:
DurableFuture<T>
public class ParallelOperation<T> extends ConcurrencyOperation<T>
Manages parallel execution of multiple branches as child context operations.

Extends ConcurrencyOperation to provide parallel-specific behavior:

  • Creates branches as ChildContextOperation with OperationSubType.PARALLEL_BRANCH
  • Checkpoints SUCCESS/FAIL on the parallel context when completion criteria are met
  • Throws ConcurrencyExecutionException when the operation fails

Context hierarchy: 

 DurableContext (root)
   └── ParallelOperation context (ChildContextOperation with PARALLEL subtype)
         ├── Branch 1 context (ChildContextOperation with PARALLEL_BRANCH)
         ├── Branch 2 context (ChildContextOperation with PARALLEL_BRANCH)
         └── Branch N context (ChildContextOperation with PARALLEL_BRANCH)

  • Method Details

    • createItem

      protected <R> ChildContextOperation<R> createItem(String operationId, String name, Function<DurableContext,R> function, TypeToken<R> resultType, SerDes serDes, DurableContextImpl parentContext)
      Description copied from class: ConcurrencyOperation
      Creates a child context operation for a single item (branch or iteration).
      Specified by:
      createItem in class ConcurrencyOperation<T>
      Type Parameters:
      R - the result type of the child operation
      Parameters:
      operationId - the unique operation ID for this item
      name - the name of this item
      function - the user function to execute
      resultType - the result type token
      serDes - the serializer/deserializer
      parentContext - the parent durable context
      Returns:
      a new ChildContextOperation

    • handleSuccess

      protected void handleSuccess(ConcurrencyCompletionStatus concurrencyCompletionStatus)
      Description copied from class: ConcurrencyOperation
      Called when the concurrency operation succeeds. Subclasses define checkpointing behavior.
      Specified by:
      handleSuccess in class ConcurrencyOperation<T>

    • handleFailure

      protected void handleFailure(ConcurrencyCompletionStatus concurrencyCompletionStatus)
      Description copied from class: ConcurrencyOperation
      Called when the concurrency operation fails. Subclasses define checkpointing and exception behavior.
      Specified by:
      handleFailure in class ConcurrencyOperation<T>

      Description copied from class: BaseDurableOperation
      Starts the operation on first execution (no existing checkpoint).

    • replay

      protected void replay(software.amazon.awssdk.services.lambda.model.Operation existing)
      Description copied from class: BaseDurableOperation
      Replays the operation from an existing checkpoint.
      Specified by:
      replay in class BaseDurableOperation<T>
      Parameters:
      existing - the checkpointed operation state

    • get

      public T get()
      Description copied from interface: DurableFuture
      Blocks until the operation completes and returns the result.

      This delegates to operation.get() which handles: - Thread deregistration (allows suspension) - Thread reactivation (resumes execution) - Result retrieval

      Returns:
      the operation result

    • validateItemCount

      protected void validateItemCount()
      Description copied from class: ConcurrencyOperation
      Validates that the number of registered items is sufficient to satisfy the completion criteria. Called at join() time because branches are registered incrementally and the total count is only known once the user calls join().
      Specified by:
      validateItemCount in class ConcurrencyOperation<T>

    • canComplete

      protected ConcurrencyCompletionStatus canComplete()
      Description copied from class: ConcurrencyOperation
      Checks whether the concurrency operation can be considered complete.
      Specified by:
      canComplete in class ConcurrencyOperation<T>
      Returns:
      the completion status if the operation is complete, or null if it should continue
